title: pachyloma arvense
intro: Pachyloma arvense, also known as Pachylaena arvense, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly found in the southern regions of South America. Traditionally, Pachyloma arvense has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pachyloma arvense include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Pachyloma arvense is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
